reasoning: Q3 has a critical semantic bug (date encoding mismatch) causing 0 output rows instead of 10. This must be fixed first. After correction, Q3 will run at ~386ms (from benchmark), but DuckDB achieves 77-112ms—a 3-4x gap remains. The evaluation identifies multiple high-impact optimization opportunities: I/O reduction (zone maps, selective columns), hash aggregation improvements, and SIMD vectorization. With 2 iterations remaining and clear bottlenecks, aggressive pursuit of all performance optimizations is justified.
focus_areas: Fix date encoding bug: Convert YYYYMMDD constants to days_since_epoch (e.g., 19950315 → 9204)
Implement zone map skipping for l_shipdate/o_orderdate to reduce I/O overhead
Optimize hash aggregation with pre-sizing (~2.5M groups, load factor 60%)
Apply SIMD vectorization to filtering and revenue calculation in Stage 3
strategy_notes: Priority order: (1) Apply critical fix immediately—change Date(19950315) to Date(9204) in line 250 of q3.cpp. (2) After correctness validation, pursue I/O optimization via zone maps and selective column loading (medium risk, 10-25% gain expected). (3) Improve hash table efficiency with better sizing and compact hash table variants (low risk, 5-15% gain). (4) Add SIMD vectorization for filtering and revenue calculation on 60M lineitem rows (high risk but 10-20% potential gain). Execute all performance optimizations in parallel where possible. Target final execution time: <200ms (vs current 386ms baseline after fix) to approach DuckDB competitiveness (~77ms represents remaining aspirational gap).
